reasoning: Panel 2-of-2 overturn of the sweep dismissal. Production genuinely diverges: handlePause (server.ts:1278-1293) re-throws validateSession's plain McpError (:276, :1285) for session-not-found, while set_breakpoint/start_debugging/step/continue convert the identical condition to structured {success:false} (server.ts:812-823, :858-866, :1082-1092). Trigger: any pause_execution call with a stale sessionId — normal MCP client input. The typed-error conversion branch at :1286-1289 is dead code because SessionNotFoundError extends McpError (debug-errors.ts:44) and is re-thrown one line earlier, corroborating accidental drift, not design; the guide's own continue example (error-handling-guide.md:89-97) contradicts actual behavior. Real + actionable (declare one envelope contract and align handlers) with severity graded down — the demote-vs-dismiss rule bars dismissal.
gray_reason: docs/error-handling-guide.md L5/L73-77 documents a 'mixed strategy', giving a defensible intent-documented dismissal; and the flagged artifact is the test file, whose assertions faithfully pin real behavior.
